item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: needpix
License: Public Domain
Title: Anteater Ant Head Free Photo
Author: falco
Release year: N/A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hair appears to glow white. Subject appears to levitate until the light

It's gone supersaiyan?!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I relate to this SCP on many levels. Constantly distressed and sensitive to light or sound. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it strangely funny how they went from a tiny bit of light to demolishing it with so much light that it vanished. But on a more serious note, this really showed the cruelty and coldness of the Foundation while keeping and interesting and lighthearted tone. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder why I never upvoted this before. I adore "No stimulus: Subject appears distressed."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We should show it Mr. Rogers Neighborhood videos. I don't think it's possible, in any universe, for Mister Rogers to hurt anyone or anything, anomaly or not..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't care for the expungement in the logs. I think a redaction'd do. I do like this though. It was one of the first ones I read when I found the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This poor lil' fragile creature has a very high risk to die at any time. Ow :-(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hair appears to glow white. Subject appears to levitate until the light is switched off. No physical damage can be noted aside from negligible burns.

Was a bit too much for me but the rest I thought was great. Poor creature, lot of testing they did.. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
NOOOOOOOOOOOOooooooo

:(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Impact with wall causes [DATA EXPUNGED], nuclear fallout continues into 3024.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
stimulus applied: an upvote

result: SCP-1364 hurled across room with explosive force, impacting with wall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I have a sad. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to adopt him

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No noticeable physical affect aside from very mild skin irritation.

Changed this to:

No noticeable physical effect aside from very mild skin irritation.

Other than that, I like this article. Definitely something that needs to be protected from the world. Aside from the stimulus tests (which are necessary, I'd say), this shows the Foundation's lighter side.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a world we live in when being lightly sprinkled with vinegar is traumatic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not changing my vote. For 231 I understand because there's a reason to be cruel, but this..this is just complete crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's nothing that real-life organizations with a goal in mind wouldn't do. It just happens that the goal here is science, not profit.

The Foundation can be unnecessarily cruel at times because it happens to be made up of humans, and humans have a tendency to be unnecessarily cruel at times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So…. the Foundation is cruel towards a sensitive anteater just because it can?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for unnecessary cruelty just in the name of SCIENCE. Seriously, it's stupid. -1

item.title: Re: I am here to deliver it.
item.content:encoded: Downvote because there is no reason to torture the creature for no reason other than to see what it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Notices the SCP number)

OH!

I see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
682 is just misunderstood.

I mean it's still a vicious unearthly monster that hates us all and wants us to die, but yeah, its nature has oft been terribly misunderstood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not how 682 works.

682 adapts to survive in an ever altering surrounding. You can't make 682 change with something that doesn't present danger to its current form.

682 doesn't copy powers. First, because SCP objects don't have powers - their anomalous nature is an integral part of them, not some external loosely-attached growth that can be just separated and tied to something else with a fancy knot. Second, because copying an ability does not automatically protect from its effects. The simplest way to adapt to an audial threat is to become deaf, not to scream that same threat at the top of… whatever 682 use in place of lungs at the time.

The continuing flanderization and misrepresentation of 682 makes me wake up at night in screams of guilt and sadness, haunted by my upvote of 682, with a voice behind my ear, whispering to me, asking if the thing 682 have been turned into still deserves to be upvoted. Some days, I don't know the answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guys, guys. Feed this thing to 682. No, really, think about it. 682 takes whatever you do to it and turns it up to eleven. So, if you feed it what is essentially the very essence of vulnerability…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This honestly doesn't do anything for me, but I'll attempt to give constructive criticism anyway.

This could just be me, but the non-light testing logs seemed extraneous. Or…something. I'm not sure what word I'm looking for.

What I do know is that I saw the first couple, went, "Okay, it just keeps getting more injured" and skipped the rest. I didn't even know light did anything special until someone on the discussion page mentioned it. Which, of course, led me to wonder why they would bother keeping it alive. Yes, I realize the Foundation is for containment and not destruction, but it honestly seemed like too much of a hassle to be worth it.

Now that I do know light does something special, I can see why they'd keep it.

So if there's a way to make the light anomaly a bit more prominent, I'd recommend doing that. It still wouldn't do anything for me, but knowing about the light influenced me to not down vote it, and it might do the same for others.

But then, I'm not an expert, so take my advice with a grain of salt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man this is so good and so sad and so adorable and even more sad

Also the anteater photo is a great choice: it looks so huggable and woebegone at the same time.

This is a really good job of a reverse 682 (in other words, it's a really good SCP in its own right).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it was!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, yes, yes yes yes. Changing it now. Also I've edited a few parts of the things that were making it unbelievable, but not too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could actually see a warmed-to-its-body-temperature saline bath being soothing for it, since that'd reduce the difference between it and its environment, but it might be advisable for there to be a dry area it can climb out into. Although then it might get cold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Might just remove that… hell, I don't know.

Maybe it uses its nose like a periscope?

But srsly… yeah… yeah… I'll polish this tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the only thing that's weirding me about this one. Also, does it need to breathe, and if so, how does it not drown? Other than that, I just feel so sorry for the poor dear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wait, it says the containment cell must be filled with saline, whichaccording to the test log is something that hurts it, explain.

item.title: Re: i love it but...
item.content:encoded: new theory, you ARE 682!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aye. 1364 is just a derpy, pathetic anteater. There are no magic tricks here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think 682 would start laughing, wipe whatever passes for tears from its face, and then eat it.

item.title: Re: i love it but...
item.content:encoded:
It's subconscious I think… I'm not actively trying to… but now that you've made me aware…

Eyes the 1682 slot.

Brb.

item.title: i love it but...
item.content:encoded:
Before you read this, remember, i REALLY like this article. i love it and would love to see more! up-voted with out question!
I love it, but really, Copernicus, what is it about you and making variations of 682? seriously, it's half of your SCPs
1. SCP-893: Regenerates from injuries
2. SCP-1124: 682 IN SPACE!
3. SCP-1203: bit of a long shot, but she does always come back from the dead.
4. SCP-682-J : 682 as a joke
and now
5. SCP-1364: the Anti-682

seriously man, i read this and immediately know who wrote it, and i love it all the same :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I'm thinking that if this is exposed to 682 both might annihilate each other or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of SCPs have scared me. Some made me laugh. This is the only one that's made me cry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the sad d'awwww. :( Poor thing. I want to hug it except that might kill it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AH! Ph, it totally is a reverse 682, isn't it? I love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can you tell me how and where the tone is off?

As per suggestions I don't think I'll change anything major, I just might add more testing logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well like I said, I don't think it's torture. They don't list many other harmful experiments, and they simply wanted to see how it reacted to fire. Now on the other hand, if they took a blowtorch to it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, that right there is enough tragedy for this piece. I think it could do with a changes in tone that make it easier for the reader to emphasize with it, but as it is I hope the little guy is not hurt by upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realize probably if it continued to hug people with its tongue it would harm itself, if it's done this more than once.

In other words its whole world is PAIN and it still manages to try and hug people.

Upvote for D'aw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As anti-682, I think it works. I just feel really sorrt for this poor little fellow and would rather not read this article anymore. :(

But really, as concept, I think you can keep it as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me sad. Don't change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this for Bright's latest challenge? Because this is totally anti-682.

Which is not a bad thing at all.

It is also an anteater. Anteaters are awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bah, screw the rest of these guys, I like it how it is. Of course, I'll probably also like the rewrite.

What can I say? I have dailysquee.com in my bookmark list, I'm a sucker for a good heart-string tugging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was interpreting it as the creature's skin was de-adapting to the light by becoming see-through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm calling PETA

On a side note, I figured that the candle test would best fit under light, to test how it reacted to "natural" light. But instead, you lit it on fire? You lit a incredebly weak and feeble animal on fire to see how it reacts, and you put it in a coma?

Now I'm really calling PETA.

Why don't you move the test log to light, and make something other than horrible injury happen to it? Otherwise, this just seems cruel, even by Foundation standards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No stimulus

Subject appears distressed.

Is it bad that I find that hilarious?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The candle test shouldn't be in the sound portion of the testing log. Other than that, this is good. I think that there is a over aboundace of incedibly powerfull god-likes on this site, and seeing the exact opposite as a pleasant change. Just because it's feeble, doesn't mean it's not Foundation worthy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see where you guys are coming from, I'm going to do a rewrite before the days end and see what happens. I might steer away from it being a 682 opposite, as novel as that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "SCP-1364 was prodded with a rake. The ensuing explosion destroyed 1364 and scattered body parts across a 500-meter radius."
